goal: Predict bulk_modulus, density, tg for PVNL (*CC(Cl)*) — PVC
assumptions: SMILES *CC(Cl)* is PVC specifically (the chloride PVNL member); exp targets: Tg 354 K, density 1.38 g/cm3, K_T 3.5-4.5 GPa.
PCFF Class II params (EMC-embedded charges) cover C/H/Cl for PVC — build-tested PASS in prior PVC2/PVC3 runs.

uncertainties.name: tg_rate_span_slope_gate
uncertainties.note: DESCOPED by user 2026-06-25: thermal reduced to SINGLE rate 25 K/ns over full 150-550 K window. Multirate slope-gate / DSC-equivalent extrapolation NOT performed — single-rate MD Tg only (reads high vs exp; partially offsets PCFF underprediction). 6.25 (32h) dropped for budget; [25,100] would only span 0.6 dec so multirate is moot without 6.25.

uncertainties.name: ff_transferability
uncertainties.note: PCFF systematically underpredicts PVC Tg (~-12%, MD ~310 vs exp 354 K) and K (~-20%, ~2.8-2.9 vs exp 3.5-4.5 GPa) — consistent sign across PVC2/PVC3, a known FF limitation, not a fit artifact. Density is reliable (~-2.5%). An OPLS-AA cross-check would test it but OPLS-AA failed to build PVC; literature_anchor (Tait/PVT K_T) already bounds the exp target.

uncertainties.name: hardware_optimum
uncertainties.note: by_forcefield.pcff default adopted (kokkos/mpi=1/1 GPU). Live host IS 4x NVIDIA A800 40GB (nvidia-smi confirmed) — the SAME host the directional_probe was measured_on (A800, 2026-06-21), so the 42.8 ns/day PCFF kokkos figure is host-matched. Cell ~3,600 atoms is within [0.5x,2x] of the 3,020-atom PCFF benchmark. hardware_benchmark probe dropped (no longer needed). Residual caveat: polymer_rules hardware_policy.values_are_benchmarked is still flagged false (the 2026-06-21 revalidation ran under CPU contention), but engine/config selection is host-matched and sound.

evidence.claim: EMC/PCFF is the validated route for PVNL; build-tested PVC PASS, PVAc PASS, PVA PASS. OPLS-AA FAILED to build PVC and PVAc, PCFF succeeded — so OPLS-AA is not an available alternative for PVC.
PCFF Class II reproduces PVC density to ~-2.5% (PVC2 1.349, PVC3 1.344 vs exp 1.38) — FF is well-parameterized for the C/H/Cl backbone.

alternatives.rejected_because: EMC build FAILED for PVC (and PVAc) on the OPLS-AA path; not a viable cross-check despite being the usual transferability probe.
retired RadonPy path; PCFF Class II gives better thermomechanical accuracy for polar vinyls.

evidence.claim: PCFF/EMC embeds bond-increment charges; PVC chloride partial charges are carried by the FF without a separate QM charge job. AM1-BCC was adequate on the retired RadonPy route (no aromatic conjugation, RESP not required).
PVC chloride gives significant partial charges on backbone carbons — PPPM required for proper long-range electrostatics. lj/cut is only for apolar UA classes (PHYC/PDIE).
DP=60/10 chains reproduced PVC density to -2.5% and gave good per-rate Tg fits in PVC3 once cooling was slowed. ~3,600 all-atom atoms is affordable on 1 GPU.
exp_tg for PVC is 354 K; sweep 250-450 K @ 20 K (narrowed from 150-550 in round 4 for budget) brackets it with margin. Glassy at 300 K (354 > 300) -> Murnaghan compression primary.

PVC3 [25,50,100] K/ns spanned only 0.6 decades -> multirate slope-gate FAILED (slope inverted -19 K, loglinear R2=0.38, VF failed), tg_method fell back to single_rate. PVC4 sets endpoints 6.25 and 100 K/ns: span = log10(100/6.25) = 1.204 decades (>= the 1.2-decade threshold to resolve the Tg-vs-ln(rate) slope against per-fit noise; slope-gate OK). n_rates = 3 >= success_criteria n_rates_min = 2.

Steps-per-T-hold hard floor is 200 ps (200000 steps at dt=1fs). At 20 K step: rate r K/ns gives N=20/(r*1e-6) steps. 100 K/ns = 200 ps (exactly at floor); 25 K/ns = 800 ps; 6.25 K/ns = 3200 ps -> all three rates clear the floor. The fast end is NOT widened: 200 K/ns = 100 ps would VIOLATE the floor and reproduce the retired-[40,160,400] degenerate c~0 fits.
evidence.value: 200-3200 ps per hold; top rate 100 K/ns at the floor

evidence.claim: Round-3 RESOURCE-BUDGET trim 5->3 rates. Cell built at 3620 atoms; measured A800 PCFF/kokkos throughput 48.5 ns/day (from completed equil logs, not the stale RTX-6000 benchmark). The 5-rate ladder over the full 150-550 K window (~130 ns) ~= 64 h on the single free GPU (GPU2; other A800s spoken for, sweeps sequential, no parallelism), exceeding the 48 h task budget. Dropping the two INTERIOR rates (12.5, 50) -> [6.25,25,100] ~= 88 ns ~= 44 h thermal + ~5 h mechanical ~= 49 h (tight, user-accepted). Removing interior points preserves the span and both endpoints, so the slope-gate span is unaffected; it costs fit DENSITY (3-point vs 5-point log-linear multirate fit). A 3-point fit is noisier, so loglinear_r_squared_min=0.90 may be harder to hit -- the accepted tradeoff. Full 150-550 K window retained (tg_t_low/high/step unchanged).
evidence.method: runtime estimate at 48.5 ns/day, 1 GPU, sequential
evidence.value: ~88 ns ~= 49 h total vs 48 h budget

evidence.claim: Round-4 WINDOW NARROW 150-550 -> 250-450 K (user decision, 2026-06-25). Total sweep time per rate = T_range / rate (independent of T_step), so the 6.25 K/ns sweep was 64 ns ~= 32 h over the 400 K window -> thermal ~43 h. Narrowing the window is the ONLY time lever that does NOT touch the rate ladder, so the slope-gate span (1.204 dec) and floor (200/800/3200 ps/hold) are UNAFFECTED -- window width does not enter the slope-gate. 250-450 K = 11 holds (was 21), cutting every sweep 2.0x -> thermal ~43 h -> ~23 h. The narrowed window still brackets the transition: it contains exp_tg 354 K AND the expected underpredicted PVC PCFF MD Tg ~310-330 K, with 4 glassy-side holds (250-310) and 7 rubbery-side holds (330-450) for the bilinear fit -> t_range_brackets_exp_tg stays satisfiable.
evidence.method: sweep time = T_range / rate; bracket check
evidence.value: 250-450 K, 11 holds, 2.0x thermal time cut, ~23 h

alternatives.rejected_because: round-2 approved 5-rate ladder; ~64 h on the single free A800 GPU exceeds the 48 h task budget. Trimmed to 3 interior-sparse rates [6.25,25,100] per user budget decision (round 3); span/endpoints preserved.
200 K/ns = 100 ps/T-hold violates the 200 ps steps-per-T-hold hard floor (the round-1 critic finding). Must widen at the slow end, not the fast end.
PVC3 default; 0.6-decade span failed the slope-gate and fell back to single_rate. PVC4 explicitly targets the slope-gate fix the memory prescribes.

decisions.choice: glassy (Murnaghan NPT compression at 300 K, primary)

evidence.claim: PVC is glassy at 300 K (exp Tg 354 > 300; is_glassy from measured Tg>300K, else glassy_hint). Murnaghan NPT compression is the validated glassy primary (Wu 2019 Comput. Mater. Sci.; symmetric, cell-size independent; Born+NVT removed for PCFF+PPPM virial incompatibility). Compression-biased pressures [-1000,0,1500,3000,5000] atm avoid tension cavitation and gave B0'=9.5 (R2=0.999), K=2.80 GPa, B_dyn=2.93 GPa (4.6% agreement) in the PVC3 revision.

gpu_vs_cpu: Murnaghan reuses the GPU pcff kokkos engine (D-08), no CPU path. rate_artifact: Murnaghan is a quasi-static EOS (no strain rate) so the deform-rate artifact that biases uniaxial K does not apply; deform (K_deform_rate 1e8/1e7 s^-1) is fallback only.

alternatives.rejected_because: fallback only; DP=60 below entanglement Me -> deform underestimates K by 30-50%. Murnaghan is less size-sensitive.

evidence.claim: FF family pcff. Cell ~= dp60 x nchain10 x 6 all-atom atoms/monomer (C2H3Cl) ~= 3,600 atoms < 10k -> 1 GPU. Within [0.5x,2x] of the 3,020-atom PCFF benchmark cell, so by_forcefield.pcff default (kokkos, mpi=1, gpu_per_run=1) adopted.
evidence.method: atoms = dp_typical x nchain x atoms_per_monomer

evidence.claim: Host-matched: live host is 4x NVIDIA A800 40GB (nvidia-smi confirmed) — the SAME host the directional_probe was measured_on (A800, 2026-06-21). PCFF kokkos full-offload recommended_by_ff = 42.8 ns/day at gpu1_mpi1, parity PASS, on this exact host. mpi=1 canonical for class2/kk (kspace on GPU). Cell ~3,600 atoms within [0.5x,2x] of the 3,020-atom benchmark cell, so the recommended config transfers directly.

Residual caveat: hardware_policy.values_are_benchmarked is still flagged false (the 2026-06-21 revalidation ran under CPU contention, suppressing absolute ns/day uniformly). Engine/config SELECTION is host-matched and parity-validated; only the absolute throughput number carries the contention caveat. No re-benchmark needed for config choice.

alternatives.rejected_because: fallback; KOKKOS full-offload is ~7.9x faster on PCFF and mpi=1 frees cores for concurrent runs.

findings: approved (round 4): the ONLY change since round-3 approval is the Tg WINDOW NARROW tg_t_high_K 550->450, tg_t_low_K 150->250 (tg_t_step_K=20 and rates [6.25,25,100] UNCHANGED). Re-verified all three gate invariants independently: (1) slope-gate UNAFFECTED — span log10(100/6.25)=1.2041 dec >= 1.2, and recomputed N=dT/(rate*dt*1e-6) for EVERY rate at dT=20K dt=1fs = 3200/800/200 ps per hold, all >= 200 ps floor (top rate 100 K/ns sits exactly at the floor, PASS >=); both depend on rates only, not window width. (2) n_rates=3 >= analyze-tg-multirate.n_rates_min=2. (3) D-01..D-08 unchanged from round-3 approved state; D-08 carries no hardware override in decided_params (policy-derived kokkos/mpi=1/1GPU == by_forcefield.pcff default), host-matched A800 — no finding.

Bracket (tg_protocol t_range_brackets_experimental_tg): narrowed 250-450 K window contains exp_tg 354 K with 96 K margin on both sides AND the expected underpredicted PVC PCFF MD Tg ~310-330 K. Recomputed holds at dT=20K: 11 total (250-450), 4 glassy-side (250-310) + 7 rubbery-side (330-450) -> enough holds each side of the transition for the bilinear fit. t_range_brackets_exp_tg stays satisfiable.

Advisory (not a defect): 250 K low-end is the tight side. Expected MD Tg ~310-330 leaves only 4 glassy holds; if the slowest-rate MD Tg dips below ~270 K (PVC3 showed a -19 K inverted-slope artifact at the fast end), the glassy branch could be under-sampled and the per-rate bilinear fit noisier on that branch. 4 holds is the bilinear minimum, not comfortable margin. Accepted under the user-approved 48 h budget; watch glassy-branch R^2 in the per-rate fits.

Advisory carried from round 3 (not a defect): 3-point log-linear multirate fit is sparser than the round-2 5-point ladder, so loglinear_r_squared_min=0.90 may be harder to hit; span/endpoints unaffected, R^2 risk is on fit density only.
round4_window_narrow: D-06_tg_ladder / D-04: post-approval WINDOW NARROW 150-550 -> 250-450 K (user decision, 2026-06-25); tg_t_step_K=20 and rates [6.25,25,100] UNCHANGED. Driver: 6.25 K/ns sweep = T_range/rate = 400K/6.25 = 64 ns ~= 32 h -> thermal ~43 h > 48 h budget. Total sweep time scales with T_range only (not T_step), so narrowing the window is the time lever that preserves the rate ladder and thus the slope-gate. 250-450 K = 11 holds (was 21) -> 2.0x cut, thermal ~43 h -> ~23 h. Slope-gate UNAFFECTED: span 1.204 dec and floor (200/800/3200 ps/hold) depend on rates, not window width. Bracket preserved: 250-450 contains exp_tg 354 + expected underpredicted MD Tg ~310-330 (4 glassy-side + 7 rubbery-side holds for the bilinear fit) -> t_range_brackets_exp_tg stays satisfiable. Status reset proposed -> re-critique the narrowed window. No other decision changed.
round3_approved: approved (round 3): user-approved budget trim of D-06_tg_ladder [6.25,12.5,25,50,100]->[6.25,25,100] is the only change since round-2 approval. Independently recomputed steps-per-T floor for all 3 rates at dt=1fs, dT=20K: N=dT/(rate*1e-6) = 3200000/800000/200000 steps = 3200/800/200 ps/hold, all >= 200ps (200000 steps) floor; top rate 100 K/ns sits exactly at the floor (PASS, >=). Span log10(100/6.25)=1.204 >= 1.2-decade slope-gate threshold; both endpoints preserved. n_rates=3 >= success_criteria.analyze-tg-multirate.n_rates_min=2.

Advisory (not a defect): dropping the two interior rates yields a sparser 3-point log-linear multirate fit, so loglinear_r_squared_min=0.90 may be harder to hit. Planner logged this as the accepted budget tradeoff. Span/endpoints are unaffected by interior thinning so the slope-gate is preserved; the R^2 risk is on fit density only.

D-08 hardware unchanged from round-2 approval: kokkos/mpi=1/1GPU == by_forcefield.pcff default == recommended_by_ff.pcff; host-matched A800 (directional_probe.measured_on); mpi=1 correct for PCFF/KOKKOS. No hw override in decided_params.
round3_budget_trim: D-06_tg_ladder: post-approval RESOURCE-BUDGET trim 5->3 rates [6.25,12.5,25,50,100] -> [6.25,25,100], dropping the two INTERIOR rates (12.5, 50). Driver: cell=3620 atoms, measured 48.5 ns/day A800 PCFF/kokkos (from completed equil logs, not the stale RTX-6000 benchmark), single free GPU (GPU2; other A800s spoken for; sweeps sequential, no parallelism) -> 5-rate full-window ~130 ns ~= 64 h > 48 h task budget; 3-rate ~88 ns ~= 44 h thermal + ~5 h mechanical ~= 49 h (tight, user-accepted). Span 1.204 dec and both endpoints PRESERVED (slope-gate OK); floor OK (3200/800/200 ps/hold, top rate at floor); n_rates=3 >= n_rates_min=2. Full 150-550 K window unchanged (tg_t_low/high/step retained). Tradeoff: sparser 3-point log-linear multirate fit (noisier; loglinear_r_squared_min=0.90 may be harder to hit). Status reset proposed->re-critique the trimmed ladder. No other decision changed; D-08 already host-matched A800 kokkos/mpi=1/1GPU.
round2_approved: approved (round 2): round-1 D-06 floor violation resolved. Independently recomputed N=tg_t_step/(rate*dt*1e-6) for all 5 rates of [6.25,12.5,25,50,100] K/ns -> 3200/1600/800/400/200 ps/hold, all >= 200 ps floor; top rate 100 K/ns sits exactly at the floor. Span log10(100/6.25)=1.20 decades clears the slope-gate threshold. D-08 pin kokkos/mpi=1/1GPU == by_forcefield.pcff default == recommended_by_ff.pcff (configs coincide); decided_params carries no hw override (policy-derived, safe); live host nvidia-smi-confirmed 4x A800 matching directional_probe.measured_on. mpi=1 correct for PCFF on KOKKOS (not the stale GPU-package anti-pattern). Advisory only: hardware_policy.host is stale (lists Quadro RTX 6000, actual A800) and values_are_benchmarked=false — bookkeeping lag in polymer_rules, not a plan defect; config selection is the policy default so require[5] does not bite.
round1_resolved: D-06_tg_ladder: tg ladder changed [12.5,25,50,100,200] -> [6.25,12.5,25,50,100]. Top rate anchored at 100 K/ns = 200 ps/T-hold (exactly at the 200 ps hard floor); span widened at the SLOW end to 6.25 K/ns (3200 ps/hold) -> log10(100/6.25)=1.20 decades. Evidence arithmetic corrected.

D-07_mechanical_regime: criteria_evaluated realigned to the property_method policy list (phase_glassy_vs_rubbery, method_physics_validity, gpu_vs_cpu, rate_artifact); added evidence covering gpu_vs_cpu and rate_artifact.

D-08_hardware: host-fact corrected — live host IS A800 (nvidia-smi), the SAME host the directional_probe was measured_on, so the 42.8 ns/day PCFF kokkos figure is host-matched. Confidence raised low->medium; hardware_benchmark probe dropped from the hardware_optimum uncertainty (reduction_probe=none); values_are_benchmarked=false caveat noted separately.
user_overrides: r5 2026-06-25: user descoped thermal to single-rate 25 K/ns over full 150-550 K window (orchestrator-applied per explicit directive; slope-gate objective dropped). Window reverted 250-450->150-550; rates [6.25,25,100]->[25].
